message:
🐛 fix(feedback): gate form behind auth check before user types (closes #9062) (#9109)

Fixes #9062

Users could fill out the entire feedback form (type, target repo,
description, screenshots) and only be told at submit time that they
needed to log in — often losing their typed draft. This change:

- Shows a prominent "Login required" gate at the top of the Submit tab
  when the user is unauthenticated (or in demo mode), with GitHub
  login / OAuth setup and open-on-GitHub actions.
- Disables all form inputs (type buttons, repo selector, description
  textarea, screenshot dropzone) via `fieldset disabled` and
  `aria-disabled` while the user is not authenticated, so they cannot
  invest typing effort before resolving login.
- Short-circuits `handleSubmit` to open the login prompt if the form
  is submitted while unauthenticated (belt-and-braces for the earlier
  Login-to-Submit button path).
- Adds three new i18n keys (`authGateTitle`, `authGateBodyDemo`,
  `authGateBodyLocal`) in `en/common.json`; other locales fall back
  to English per the project's i18n policy.

Reuses existing UI primitives (`Button`) and the existing
`LoginPromptDialog` flow — no new modals or magic numbers introduced.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1929,6 +1929,9 @@
     "submitShortcutHint": "Press Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to submit.",
     "loginBannerDemo": "Login with GitHub to submit feedback and manage requests",
     "loginBannerLocal": "Set up GitHub OAuth to submit feedback, earn coins, and track requests",
+    "authGateTitle": "Login required to submit feedback",
+    "authGateBodyDemo": "This is a public demo — GitHub login requires your own KubeStellar Console instance. Log in or install locally before filling out the form so your draft is not lost.",
+    "authGateBodyLocal": "Set up a GitHub OAuth app (or log in if already configured) before filling out the form so your draft is not lost.",
     "requestSubmitted": "Request Submitted!",
     "status": {
       "open": "Open",
